title: Mementos.
content:
Arranging long-locked drawers and shelves
    Of cabinets, shut up for years,
    What a strange task we've set ourselves!
    How still the lo

title: Winter Stores.
content:
We take from life one little share,
    And say that this shall be
    A space, redeemed from toil and care,
    From tears and sadness free.

title: Pilate's Wife's Dream by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
I've quenched my lamp, I struck it in that start
Which every limb convulsed, I heard it fall­
The crash blent with my sleep, I saw depart

title: Speak Of The North! A Lonely Moor by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Speak of the North! A lonely moor
Silent and dark and tractless swells,
The waves of some wild streamlet pour
Hurriedly through its ferny dells.

title: Life by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
LIFE, believe, is not a dream
So dark as sages say;
Oft a little morning rain
Foretells a pleasant day.
Sometimes there are clouds of gloom,
But these

title: The Wife's Will by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
SIT still­a word­a breath may break
(As light airs stir a sleeping lake,)
The glassy calm that soothes my woes,
The sweet, the deep, the full repose.

title: The Wife's Will by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:

title: The Teacher's Monologue by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
The room is quiet, thoughts alone
People its mute tranquillity;
The yoke put on, the long task done,­
I am, as it is bliss to be,
Still and untroubled

title: Passion by Charlotte Bronte — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
SOME have won a wild delight,
By daring wilder sorrow;
Could I gain thy love to-night,
I'd hazard death to-morrow.

title: Presentiment.
content:

    Come to the hearth awhile;
    The wind so wildly sweeps away,
    The clouds so darkly pile.

title: On The Death Of Anne Bronte
content:
There's little joy in life for me,
    And little terror in the grave;
    I 've lived the parting hour to see

content:

title: Regret.
content:
Long ago I wished to leave
    "The house where I was born;"
    Long ago I used to grieve,
    My home seemed so forlorn.

title: The Wood.
content:

title: Frances.
content:

title: Parting.
content:
There's no use in weeping,
    Though we are condemned to part:
    There's such a thing as keeping
    A remembrance in one's heart:

title: The Missionary.
content:

title: Stanzas.
content:

title: The Welcome Home. (From Gilbert)
content:
Above the city hangs the moon,
    Some clouds are boding rain;

    To-night comes home again.

title: The Parlour. (From Gilbert)
content:

title: Preference.
content:

title: Pleasure
content:
A Short Poem or Else Not Say I

    True pleasure breathes not city air,

title: The Letter.
content:
What is she writing? Watch her now,
    How fast her fingers move!
    How eagerly her youthful brow
    Is bent in thought above!

title: Apostasy.
content:
This last denial of my faith,
    Thou, solemn Priest, hast heard;
    And, though upon my bed of death,
    I call not back a word.
    Point
